---
title: "Data Frames"
# Purpose: Teach beginner data frame inspection, column access, and simple summaries.
---

{{< include ../_extensions/r-wasm/live/_knitr.qmd >}}

<!-- Purpose: This lesson uses the bundled CSV to introduce rectangular plant data. -->

A data frame stores data in rows and columns. A common ecology workflow is to
read a CSV file, inspect it, and summarize a measurement by group.

## Read the CSV

```{webr}
#| autorun: true
plants <- read.csv("data/plant_growth.csv")

head(plants)
str(plants)
```

## Work with columns

The `$` operator selects one column.

```{webr}
plants <- read.csv("data/plant_growth.csv")

plants$height_cm
mean(plants$height_cm)
max(plants$leaf_count)
```

## Summarize by treatment

`aggregate()` is a useful Base R function for grouped summaries.

```{webr}
plants <- read.csv("data/plant_growth.csv")

aggregate(height_cm ~ treatment, data = plants, FUN = mean)
aggregate(leaf_count ~ treatment, data = plants, FUN = mean)
```

## Filter rows

Use a logical condition to select rows.

```{webr}
plants <- read.csv("data/plant_growth.csv")

late_plants <- plants[plants$day == 21, ]
late_plants
```
